last_item: **(P2 · W0) Enforce the full ephemeral-`.claude/` gitignore set fleet-wide + in `doctor` + template.** Broadened 2026-05-29 from the events.jsonl-only case after a second instance of the same class. The bug class: a per-machine ephemeral `.claude/` runtime file that is either untracked-but-unignored (autostash `--include-untracked` grabs it, pop fails → orphan) or outright tracked (the daemon deletes/rewrites it each tick → that tracked change is autostashed and fails to pop → orphan). Concrete instances found: (i) `.claude/backlog-agent-events.jsonl` — gitignored in `backlog-infra` only, untracked elsewhere; fixed during bring-up (added + pushed to 6 repos, stashes dropped). (ii) `.claude/agent-cooldown.json` — **tracked** in dark-mode-safari + sacred-geography (deleted on cooldown-clear → one orphan stash per tick; dark-mode-safari hit 4, sacred-geography 7); fixed 2026-05-29 (untrack + gitignore + push; see Done). **Note `agent-cooldown.json` is missing from `templates/dev-project/.gitignore` entirely.** (iii) `.claude/backlog-agent-failcounts.json` — listed in the template but **untracked-unignored** in backlog-dashboard, dark-mode-safari, predictive-history, sacred-geography (latent autostash co-trigger); only sacred-geography fixed so far. Enforcement: (a) define the canonical ephemeral set in one place and have `doctor` assert every active repo ignores all of it (currently it checks only a subset) — hard-fail on a *tracked* ephemeral file, warn on untracked-unignored; (b) make `templates/dev-project/.gitignore` the superset (add `agent-cooldown.json`); (c) one-shot sweep the remaining repos for the failcounts line. → ties to the D1 telemetry bus (W1), which removes the deeper "tracked status/history" variant (see TaizMail item below).
